caller_config.derivation: Unchanged from v1. Quality-weighted base frequencies, strand bias filter, Q20 pileup base quality gate.

pileup_config.derivation: Phred Q20 = 1% error rate. Bases below excluded before counting.

substrate.pileup: CPU with Q20 base quality filter
substrate.adaptive_dispatch_note: Per-read GPU SmithWaterman dispatch measured at 344min vs 26min CPU for 36bp reads (13x slower). Kernel launch overhead (~5-20μs × 7.5M reads) dominates. GPU mapping reserved for reads ≥100bp.

discovery.trio_status: unavailable (rhizoCrypt: TCP-only no UDS, loamSpine: runtime panic)
discovery.degradation: local session IDs, BLAKE3 content hashes preserved

REL1164M.note: Over-calling: 78 sovereign vs 4 breseq. Strict caller filters real low-frequency noise but also some real variants.

REL7177M.note: No breseq baseline available for this clone.

REL8593M.note: Parity improving: 2.1x ratio. Lower depth (20x) may explain fewer sovereign calls.

REL10379.note: UNDER-calling: breseq finds 253 variants, we find 63. At high generations with many real mutations, our strict thresholds filter real variants. Frequency threshold 0.75 may be too strict for sub-clonal sweeps.

REL10926.note: UNDER-calling confirmed: 81 vs 258. Same pattern as REL10379. breseq uses polymorphism mode for these late-generation samples.

parity_evolution.diagnosis: Massive over-calling: noise counted as variants
99.8% FP reduction. Still over-calling at low generations, under-calling at high generations.

parity_evolution.config: Same as v1 + adaptive GPU dispatch + connect-probe discovery
parity_evolution.diagnosis: Full 7-clone run confirms crossover: early clones over-call (strict threshold catches noise but misses true variants), late clones under-call (accumulated mutations filtered by 0.75 frequency gate). Next: tune frequency to generation or adopt breseq-like polymorphism mode.

computation.substrate: Adaptive CPU mapping + GPU pileup scan + GPU SNP calling
